title: Traided
description: AI-powered trading automation platform. Describe your strategies in natural language, and our system executes them 24/7.
goal: Let anyone automate trading strategies without writing code. Translate plain-English strategy descriptions into executable trading logic that runs 24/7.
highlights: Natural language strategy parsing via LLM
content: Traided was born from a simple question: why do you need to write code to automate a trading strategy? Most traders have ideas but lack the technical skills to execute them automatically. The platform bridges that gap — describe your strategy in natural language, and the system translates it into executable trading logic that runs around the clock. Built with Python and FastAPI on the backend, React on the frontend, and leveraging LLMs for strategy parsing.

title: DLetter
description: AI-powered service that transforms thousands of Discord messages into clean, daily digest summaries delivered to your inbox each morning.
goal: Turn Discord noise into signal. Make it possible to stay on top of active servers without scrolling through thousands of messages.
highlights: Discord bot with real-time message ingestion
content: Discord servers generate thousands of messages daily. Miss a day and you're hopelessly behind. DLetter watches the servers you care about, summarizes the key discussions with AI, and delivers a clean digest to your inbox each morning. Signal instead of noise.

title: Liatch
description: Platform connecting companies, schools, and students for LIA internships. Making it easy to find the right placement, talent, and support — all in one place.
goal: Simplify the LIA internship process for Swedish vocational students. Connect schools, students, and companies on one platform.
content: LIA (Lärande i Arbete) internships are required for Swedish YH students, but the matching process is chaotic — schools, students, and companies all juggling spreadsheets and emails. Liatch brings the three parties together in one place. Schools track their students, students find placements, companies source talent, all with built-in communication and oversight.

title: Nordic Wealth Network
description: Account management and business development at a Nordic capital advisory. Owning the full sales cycle — prospecting, relationship building, deal coordination.
goal: Own the full sales cycle at a Nordic capital advisory. Build pipeline with family offices, pension funds, and long-term capital providers. Match growth-stage companies with investors whose horizons and values align.
highlights: Outbound prospecting and relationship building across Nordic institutional investors
Pipeline management and weekly reporting for family offices and funds
Coordinated investor introductions between companies and capital partners
content: At Nordic Wealth Network I own the full cycle: outreach, qualifying, nurturing, and coordinating deal introductions. The work is about trust and alignment — matching companies with the right long-term capital, not just any capital. Specific outcomes are under NDA, but the pipeline is real and the relationships are being built.

title: Catery
description: Co-founded and ran business development at a foodtech startup. Closed restaurants onto the platform, pitched corporate buyers, and shaped the go-to-market motion.
goal: Build the supply side (restaurants) and demand side (corporate catering buyers) from zero. Own outreach, positioning, partnership deals, and the messaging that turned a two-sided marketplace into an actual business.
highlights: Co-founded and led all business development from day one
Pitched corporate buyers and built the outbound sales motion
Shaped brand positioning and go-to-market strategy
content: As co-founder, I ran business development end-to-end. Cold outreach to restaurants, pitching corporate buyers, negotiating partnership terms, and constantly refining how the product was positioned. The technical side was handled by my co-founder — my job was to prove people wanted this, then go get them. The venture wound down in 2025 to free up focus for Avendavi, but the lesson carries: you learn what a market actually needs by getting rejected by it first.
